title: The Summer Movie Awards | The Big Picture (Ep. 81)
format: Sean Fennessey and Chris Ryan close out summer 2018 with a run of movie awards: best scene, best sequel, biggest missed opportunity, rookie of the summer, landing on Mission: Impossible - Fallout as the best of the season.

films.title: Set It Up
films.note: The office consensus pick. Sean calls it the lightest and funniest of the bunch; Zoey Deutch and Glen Powell star, both relative unknowns.

films.title: To All the Boys I've Loved Before
films.note: The other half of Netflix's rom-com run, out days earlier, minting Lana Condor and Noah Centineo overnight.

films.title: Crazy Rich Asians
films.note: Its opening weekend is the theatrical leg of the same rom-com revival.

films.title: The Cloverfield Paradox
films.note: The blockbuster-swing strategy Chris expected from Netflix 18 months ago, dropped after the Super Bowl.

films.title: Bright
films.note: The other big-budget JJ Abrams-era bet, only from December.

films.title: Outlaw King
films.note: Coming this fall: David Mackenzie's Robert the Bruce film, part of Netflix filling genres Hollywood dropped.

films.title: Hold the Dark
films.note: Jeremy Saulnier's thriller, another fall release in the same slate.

films.title: Mudbound
films.note: The earlier, prestige-chasing Netflix strategy: the awards play.

films.title: War Machine
films.note: Same prestige era; a swing that didn't connect.

films.title: Tully
films.note: Chris's pick for Charlize Theron: a vanity-free turn he rates near Monster, matched to Diablo Cody's dialogue.

films.title: Don't Worry, He Won't Get Far on Foot
films.note: Sean's pick for Jonah Hill as an AA sponsor in Gus Van Sant's film: svelte, blond, a real transformation few people saw.

films.title: Christopher Robin
films.note: Sean's shout-out to Jim Cummings, the longtime voice of Winnie the Pooh.

films.title: First Reformed
films.note: Ethan Hawke, likely a nominee, in one of the best performances both hosts have seen in a while.

films.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp
films.note: Chris's pick: Michael Peña, the most delightful thing in the Marvel universe, stealing every scene as he did in the first film.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: Sean floats two: Josh Brolin bringing pathos to Thanos, and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor as Ebony Maw, the zombie-alien twerp who wins a scene against three Oscar-nominated actors.

films.title: Incredibles 2
films.note: Sean's pick, now the biggest animated movie ever made and, he argues, a rare useful sequel.

films.title: Sicario: Day of the Soldado
films.note: Chris's pick: darker, more violent, taking the first film's uncomfortable parts to unbearable levels. He loved it and won't recommend it to anyone.

films.title: Hereditary
films.note: Sean's pick, though he takes Ari Aster's line that it's more an adult tragedy than a horror movie until the final 20 minutes.

films.title: The First Purge
films.note: Sean found it effective.

films.title: Truth or Dare
films.note: Sean was not into it; Chris found it fun.

films.title: Unfriended: Dark Web
films.note: Both thought it was good.

films.title: Upgrade
films.note: More sci-fi than horror, a little Verhoeven.

films.title: The Ritual
films.note: Chris's recommendation: a David Bruckner film with Rafe Spall, hikers meeting something in the Scandinavian woods.

films.title: Ghost Stories
films.note: Chris's other pick: a Martin Freeman-led anthology about a paranormal debunker, with a touch of Ben Wheatley.

films.title: Three Identical Strangers
films.note: Triplets separated at birth reunite by chance; the first 12 minutes are the setup and everything after is wild and upsetting. Being adapted as a fictional film.

films.title: Minding the Gap
films.note: Bing Liu's Hulu doc, sometimes called Hoop Dreams for skateboarding: three Rockford kids over a decade, with Steve James producing.

films.title: Gemini
films.note: Chris's pick: Aaron Katz's LA mystery with Lola Kirke and Zoe Kravitz, essentially The Long Goodbye by way of modern celebrity.

films.title: Sorry to Bother You
films.note: Boots Riley's anarchic capitalism satire with Lakeith Stanfield.

films.title: Leave No Trace
films.note: Debra Granik's father-daughter drama with Ben Foster, and a breakout for Thomasin McKenzie.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout
films.note: The all-white bathroom fight: choreographed well, funny, and it introduces characters smartly. Sean has seen the film twice for it.

films.title: Sicario: Day of the Soldado
films.note: Chris's pick: the border crossing shot from a child's perspective, plus the double-cross scenes. Harrowing stuff.

films.title: Eighth Grade
films.note: The pool-party sequence, scored to throbbing EDM; Bo Burnham has called it among the hardest in the movie to shoot.

films.title: The Meg
films.note: Sean's pick: a Statham shark movie that should have been an easy win, made cheap. A sequel is coming anyway.

films.title: Ocean's 8
films.note: Inert, unfunny, a heist that never quite comes together. Sandra Bullock is underused, and both wish Soderbergh had directed it.

films.title: Skyscraper
films.note: Die Hard in a super-building, the easiest sell there is, and it doesn't work. A rough summer for the Rock alongside Rampage.

segments.heading: Things That Were Missing From This Summer
segments.note: Chris's category: the movies that never showed up

films.title: Deadpool 2
films.note: The one that did show up. Not a story or characters Sean cares about, but the slavish commitment to the fourth-wall bit stuck with him.

films.title: Baby Driver
films.note: The kind of low-stakes, high-reward smart summer movie that gave last year something to chew on.

films.title: War for the Planet of the Apes
films.note: Another 2017 entry in the 'pretty good, I'd see that again' lane that 2018 lacked.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: No big, serious, prestige-yet-popular swing this summer.

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: No Nolan-style event movie this time around.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout
films.note: Vanessa Kirby, going toe-to-toe with Tom Cruise in her first big movie.

films.title: Crazy Rich Asians
films.note: Awkwafina, off the double shot of this and Ocean's 8.

films.title: Eighth Grade
films.note: Elsie Fisher.

films.title: Leave No Trace
films.note: Thomasin McKenzie, the real revelation of the film.

films.title: Madeline's Madeline
films.note: Helena Howard in Josephine Decker's experimental film about an acting student.

films.title: Blockers
films.note: Geraldine Viswanathan, who owns the movie; next up is Bad Education opposite Hugh Jackman.

films.title: To All the Boys I've Loved Before
films.note: Lana Condor, in a specific, idiosyncratic Meg Ryan-style lead.

films.title: Solo: A Star Wars Story
films.note: A Star Wars movie almost no one liked. Ron Howard didn't save it, the origin story shouldn't have been an origin story, and the public production drama hurt it. Still, three months on, both admit it's not bad, just not good.

segments.heading: Worst Movie of the Summer
segments.note: and the sorry state of comedy

films.title: The Happytime Murders
films.note: Sean's pick, no contest. Brian Henson's dirty-puppet movie with Melissa McCarthy: good puppeteering, painfully unfunny.

films.title: Tag
films.note: Cited in the comedy-is-broken conversation: funny parts, but not enough effort.

films.title: Game Night
films.note: The closest thing this year to an actually-written comedy; a good script that gets hairy at the end.

films.title: Blockers
films.note: The other comedy that emerged with a solid box office and real support.

films.title: Won't You Be My Neighbor?
films.note: Morgan Neville's Mr. Rogers doc, more tribute than biopic. The only movie that made Sean weep in theaters this year.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout
films.note: The unanimous pick. Chris McQuarrie understands the spirit of a summer movie: a strong star, well-built set pieces, real tension, and a rewatchability rare for the season.

To All the Boys I've Loved Before.title: To All the Boys I've Loved Before

Don't Worry, He Won't Get Far on Foot.title: Don't Worry, He Won't Get Far on Foot

War for the Planet of the Apes.title: War for the Planet of the Apes
